methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Deep Isolation is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $26M to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power, though the narrow peer set constrains certain methodologies.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, anchors valuation to the $26M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $52M. A peer-median approach was not viable: while 8 same-sector comparables exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier transactions yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making a narrow comp median unreliable. Using an all-sector median would introduce material noise.

The triangulated range is $44M (low) to $60M (high), with a median of $52M. This range reflects the single applicable methodology and carries material uncertainty given the narrow comparable dataset in nuclear energy and the absence of direct, same-stage transaction evidence. Key sensitivities include the actual step-up multiplier for this subsector—nuclear infrastructure plays may command different premium than generalized venture benchmarks—and Deep Isolation's specific technology differentiation relative to the identified peer cohort.
